source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: Unable to withdraw Kerosene from vaultmanagerv2::withdraw as it expects a vault.oracle() method which is missing in Kerosene vaults
summary: VaultManagerV2 has one withdraw function responsible for withdrawing both exogenous collateral (weth/wsteth) and endogenous collateral (Kerosene). However, the function expects the vault passed as an argument to have an oracle method. This is the case for Vault contracts, but not the case for the BoundedKerosineVault or UnboundedKerosineVault contracts. This means that whenever a user attempts to withdraw Kerosene deposited into the contract the call will revert, meaning the Kerosene remains stuck in the contract permanently.
root_cause: missing validation or protection in Unable to withdraw Kerosene from vaultmanagerv2::withdraw as it expects a vault.oracle() method which is missing in Kerosene vaults, leaving the affected path without the required safety check
broken_invariants: oracle valuation must remain correct under the root cause: missing validation or protection in Unable to withdraw Kerosene from vaultmanagerv2::withdraw as it expects a vault.oracle() method which is missing in Kerosene vaults, leaving the affected path without the required safety check
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: VaultManagerV2 has one withdraw function responsible for withdrawing both exogenous collateral (weth/wsteth) and endogenous collateral (Kerosene). However, the function expects the vault passed as an argument to have an oracle method. This is the case for Vault contracts, but not the case for the BoundedKerosineVault or UnboundedKerosineVault contracts. This means that whenever a user attempts to withdraw Kerosene deposited into the contract the call will revert, meaning the Kerosene remains stuck in the contract permanently.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Given that the value of exogenous and endogenous collateral is calculated differently it is necessary to handle withdrawal of exogenous collateral and Kerosene differently. It would avoid added complexity to the function logic to have two different withdraw and withdrawKerosene functions.
